summary: Matched most of Captain Falcon's `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` by refactoring duplicated collision-transition logic for grounded landing from aerial Falcon Kick states, tightening GObj parameter types, adding a Captain forward declaration include, and marking several scratch entries as OK. The PR report shows the file at 85.76% matched, with many functions at 100% and remaining mismatch concentrated in a few physics/animation/collision routines.
what_changed: Added `#include "ftCaptain/forward.h"` to `ftCa_SpecialLw.c`, likely to support using `ftCaptain_MotionState` in a helper signature.
Introduced `static void resetCmdAndThrow(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` to centralize clearing `fp->cmd_vars[0..2]` and `fp->throw_flags`.

Introduced `static void doColl(Fighter_GObj* gobj, ftCaptain_MotionState msid)` to centralize the collision transition path: check `ft_80081D0C(gobj) != GA_Ground`, get fighter/data attributes, reset command/throw state, call `ftCommon_8007D7FC(fp)`, and call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` with `da->speciallw_landing_lag_mul`.
Rewrote `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Coll` to use `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` and delegate to `doColl(gobj, ftCa_MS_SpecialAirLwEnd)`.
Rewrote `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEndAir_Coll` similarly to use `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` and delegate to `doColl(gobj, ftCa_MS_SpecialAirLwEnd)`.
Changed some collision callback parameter types from `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*` where the implementation treats the object as a fighter object.

Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` entries from percentages to `OK` for `ftCo_800C4724`, `func_800E4040`, `done_fork__func_800E4040`, `func_800e415c`, `ftCa_SpecialLwEndAir_Coll`, and `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEndAir_Coll`.
smart_moves: Factoring repeated command-variable and throw-flag clearing into `resetCmdAndThrow` reduced duplicated statements and helped the compiler shape match across related collision functions.

Factoring the common landing collision transition into `doColl` preserved one canonical sequence for `ft_80081D0C`, `ftCommon_8007D7FC`, and `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, which is useful when several Falcon Kick collision callbacks share nearly identical behavior.
Using a motion-state parameter (`ftCaptain_MotionState msid`) made the helper reusable without hard-coding a single state, while still matching the observed call sites in this diff.
Explicit `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` calls were used as a matching tactic to reproduce stack layout around the helper-based collision callbacks.
Replacing numeric scratch match percentages with `OK` records kept the scratch database aligned with newly matched or accepted functions.
decomp_lessons: When two fighter callbacks have the same landing-transition body, extracting a static helper can improve matching and avoid small ordering/stack differences between copies.

For fighter-specific code, prefer `Fighter_GObj*` in helper and callback signatures when the function immediately uses `GET_FIGHTER`; this can clarify intent and support typed motion-state helper signatures.

Preserve exact side-effect order in collision transitions: collision state check, fighter/dat_attrs access, stack padding if needed, command/throw reset, common transition helper, then `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
Landing-lag multipliers stored in character data attributes (`speciallw_landing_lag_mul`) should be passed directly into `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` when matching special-move landing transitions.
Scratch status updates may cover adjacent or previously forked function entries when a refactor proves byte-equivalent enough to mark them `OK`.
naming_conventions: `resetCmdAndThrow` uses lower camel case for a file-local helper that clears `cmd_vars` and `throw_flags`.
`doColl` is a short file-local helper name for shared collision transition behavior.

Existing Captain Falcon special-low functions follow the pattern `ftCa_Special..._{Anim,Phys,Coll}` and distinguish grounded/air/end variants with names such as `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Coll` and `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEndAir_Coll`.
Motion-state enum values follow the `ftCa_MS_...` prefix convention, e.g. `ftCa_MS_SpecialAirLwEnd`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` in both the helper and wrapper collision functions to influence stack layout for matching.
Reordered/reset `cmd_vars` assignment into a chained assignment `fp->cmd_vars[0] = fp->cmd_vars[1] = fp->cmd_vars[2] = 0;`, replacing explicit reverse-order stores from the old code.
Changed the collision test from a truthy `ft_80081D0C(gobj)` style in the removed code to `ft_80081D0C(gobj) != GA_Ground` inside the shared helper.
Introduced static helpers rather than inlining duplicated callback bodies, suggesting function extraction was required or beneficial for the generated code shape.
Used typed `Fighter_GObj*` parameters for functions that operate on fighter objects, while `ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Coll` still calls `ft_80084104(gobj)` with the existing `HSD_GObj*` signature.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish the remaining mismatches in `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Phys`, `ftCa_SpecialLw_Phys`, `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialLw_Coll`, and `ftCa_SpecialLw_Anim`.
Search for other fighter special-move collision callbacks that can reuse the `resetCmdAndThrow` plus `doColl` helper pattern.
Check whether `func_800E4040` and `func_800e415c` were later renamed to descriptive `ftCa_` names after being marked OK in scratches.
Compare generated assembly before and after `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` in these callbacks to document why stack padding was necessary.
